meta.description: Compressed hunt cards for general-infrastructure — one-line detection rules for bulk scanning
meta.usage: Load cards into context. For each card, grep target code for card.grep. On hit: execute card.check steps directly, use validWhen/invalidWhen/impact for rapid triage, and only read full DB entry (card.ref + card.lines) for confirmed true/likely positives.

cards.title: Proxy Pattern Implementation Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Proxy pattern vulnerabilities stem from the separation of contract logic (implementation) and state (proxy), combined...
check: VERIFY: Proxy pattern vulnerabilities stem from the separation of contract logic (implementation) and state (proxy), combined with the use of delegatecall...
cards.validWhen: Proxy pattern vulnerabilities stem from the separation of contract logic (implementation) and state (proxy), combined with the use of delegatecall for forwarding calls.

cards.title: VULNERABILITY PATTERNS
cards.detect: Implementation contract constructor doesn't disable initializers
check: VERIFY: Implementation contract constructor doesn't disable initializers
FALSIFY: Use initializer instead of constructor
cards.antipattern: Implementation contract constructor doesn't disable initializers
cards.validWhen: Implementation contract constructor doesn't disable initializers

cards.title: Vulnerability Description
cards.detect: UUPS proxy vulnerabilities arise from several fundamental issues:
check: VERIFY: UUPS proxy vulnerabilities arise from several fundamental issues
PROVE: *Scenario 1: Uninitialized Implementation Takeover (MEDIUM-HIGH); Protocol deploys UUPS proxy with OZ 4.3.1 but forgets to initialize implementation contract
ANTIPATTERN: Implementation contract not initialized after deployment
cards.antipattern: Implementation contract not initialized after deployment
cards.validWhen: UUPS proxy vulnerabilities arise from several fundamental issues

cards.title: Vulnerability Description
cards.detect: Diamond proxy vulnerabilities arise from several fundamental issues:
check: VERIFY: Diamond proxy vulnerabilities arise from several fundamental issues
PROVE: *Scenario 1: Storage Collision Leading to System Failure (HIGH); Protocol upgrades Diamond with new struct that changes size from 7 to 2 slots
ANTIPATTERN: Changing struct size shifts all subsequent storage
cards.antipattern: Changing struct size shifts all subsequent storage
cards.validWhen: Diamond proxy vulnerabilities arise from several fundamental issues

check: VERIFY: Proxy variables collide with implementation's storage layout.

cards.title: Vulnerability Categories
cards.detect: State updates occur AFTER external calls, allowing recursive exploitation.
check: VERIFY: State updates occur AFTER external calls, allowing recursive exploitation.
cards.validWhen: State updates occur AFTER external calls, allowing recursive exploitation.

check: VERIFY: State update AFTER external call

cards.antipattern: Oracle queries pool during withdrawal callback

cards.title: 1. ERC-677 `onTokenTransfer` Reentrancy During Liquidation
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because Aave V2 forks deployed on chains using ERC-677 bridged tokens (like Gnosis Chain)...
check: VERIFY: Aave V2 forks deployed on chains using ERC-677 bridged tokens (like Gnosis Chain) inherit a callback mechanism not originally accounted...
PROVE: Attacker creates a position with health factor barely above 1; Wait for interest accrual to push health factor below 1
ANTIPATTERN: Aave V2 fork on Gnosis Chain uses ERC-677 bridged tokens
cards.antipattern: Aave V2 fork on Gnosis Chain uses ERC-677 bridged tokens
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because Aave V2 forks deployed on chains using ERC-677 bridged tokens (like Gnosis Chain) inherit a callback mechanism not originally accounted...

cards.title: 2. Flash Loan Callback Reentrancy — Deposit-in-Callback
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because the flash loan function calls back to the borrower BEFORE verifying that the loan has...
check: VERIFY: flash loan function calls back to the borrower BEFORE verifying that the loan has been repaid.
PROVE: flash() calls back BEFORE checking repayment
cards.antipattern: flash() calls back BEFORE checking repayment
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because the flash loan function calls back to the borrower BEFORE verifying that the loan has been repaid.

cards.title: 3. ERC-1155 `onERC1155Received` Reentrancy During NFT Mint
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because ERC-1155 token transfers trigger `onERC1155Received()` callbacks on the recipient.
check: VERIFY: ERC-1155 token transfers trigger onERC1155Received() callbacks on the recipient.
PROVE: ERC-1155 mint triggers onERC1155Received callback
cards.antipattern: ERC-1155 mint triggers onERC1155Received callback
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because ERC-1155 token transfers trigger onERC1155Received() callbacks on the recipient.

cards.title: 4. Native ETH/AVAX `receive()` Reentrancy — exitMarket/State Bypass
cards.detect: This vulnerability exists because native token transfers (ETH, AVAX) trigger the recipient's `receive()` or...
check: VERIFY: native token transfers (ETH, AVAX) trigger the recipient's receive() or fallback() function.
PROVE: StarsArena sends AVAX during share operations
cards.antipattern: StarsArena sends AVAX during share operations
cards.validWhen: This vulnerability exists because native token transfers (ETH, AVAX) trigger the recipient's receive() or fallback() function.

cards.title: 1. Curve Pool Read-Only Reentrancy
cards.detect: Curve's `remove_liquidity()` function sends native ETH to the caller via `raw_call()` **before** updating the pool's internal balances.
check: VERIFY: Curve's remove_liquidity() function sends native ETH to the caller via raw_call() before updating the pool's internal balances.
PROVE: Flash loan massive amounts of ETH/WETH and other assets; Add liquidity to Curve pool (e.g., stETH/ETH) to obtain LP tokens
ANTIPATTERN: dForce reads Curve LP token price via oracle during reentrancy
cards.antipattern: dForce reads Curve LP token price via oracle during reentrancy
cards.validWhen: Curve's remove_liquidity() function sends native ETH to the caller via raw_call() before updating the pool's internal balances.

cards.title: 2. Balancer Pool Read-Only Reentrancy
cards.detect: Balancer's `exitPool()` function sends native ETH to the caller before updating the pool's internal share accounting.
check: VERIFY: Balancer's exitPool() function sends native ETH to the caller before updating the pool's internal share accounting.
PROVE: Sturdy reads B-stETH-STABLE price from oracle during exitPool callback
cards.antipattern: Sturdy reads B-stETH-STABLE price from oracle during exitPool callback
cards.validWhen: Balancer's exitPool() function sends native ETH to the caller before updating the pool's internal share accounting.

cards.title: 1. Read-Only Reentrancy via Curve LP Withdrawal
cards.detect: Curve pools use ETH transfers (via `call.value()`) during `remove_liquidity()`.
check: VERIFY: Curve pools use ETH transfers (via call.value()) during remove_liquidity().
PROVE: Flash loan massive ETH from 7+ sources (Balancer, Aave, Radiant, UniV3, Sushi x3, Zyber, SwapFlashLoan); Deposit ETH into Curve wstETH/ETH pool → receive LP tokens
ANTIPATTERN: dForce prices wstETHCRV-gauge using Curve virtual price
cards.antipattern: dForce prices wstETHCRV-gauge using Curve virtual price
cards.validWhen: Curve pools use ETH transfers (via call.value()) during remove_liquidity().

cards.title: 2. Read-Only Reentrancy via Curve Callback (Conic $3.25M)
cards.detect: Conic Finance's ETH omnipool used Curve LP tokens as underlying.
check: VERIFY: Conic Finance's ETH omnipool used Curve LP tokens as underlying.
PROVE: Conic ETH omnipool prices LP tokens using Curve reserves
cards.antipattern: Conic ETH omnipool prices LP tokens using Curve reserves
cards.validWhen: Conic Finance's ETH omnipool used Curve LP tokens as underlying.

cards.title: 3. Balancer View Reentrancy via LP Price Oracle (Sentiment $1M)
cards.detect: Sentiment protocol used Balancer weighted LP tokens as collateral.
check: VERIFY: Sentiment protocol used Balancer weighted LP tokens as collateral.
PROVE: Sentiment uses Balancer LP getPrice() as collateral oracle
cards.antipattern: Sentiment uses Balancer LP getPrice() as collateral oracle
cards.validWhen: Sentiment protocol used Balancer weighted LP tokens as collateral.

cards.title: 4. Fake Token Reentrancy in DEX Swap (Orion $645K)
cards.detect: Orion Protocol's `swapThroughOrionPool()` function allowed users to specify arbitrary token addresses in the swap path.
check: VERIFY: Orion Protocol's swapThroughOrionPool() function allowed users to specify arbitrary token addresses in the swap path.
PROVE: swapThroughOrionPool allows arbitrary token in path
cards.antipattern: swapThroughOrionPool allows arbitrary token in path
cards.validWhen: Orion Protocol's swapThroughOrionPool() function allowed users to specify arbitrary token addresses in the swap path.

cards.title: 5. Native Token Receive() Reentrancy (StarsArena $3M)
cards.detect: StarsArena (a social token trading platform on Avalanche) sent AVAX via `call.value()` during share selling.
check: VERIFY: StarsArena (a social token trading platform on Avalanche) sent AVAX via call.value() during share selling.
PROVE: Share selling sends AVAX before updating state
cards.antipattern: Share selling sends AVAX before updating state
cards.validWhen: StarsArena (a social token trading platform on Avalanche) sent AVAX via call.value() during share selling.

cards.title: 6. Curve Reentrancy in Read-Only Oracle (Sturdy $800K)
cards.detect: Sturdy Finance (Aave V2 fork) used Curve stETH LP tokens as collateral, pricing them via Curve's `get_virtual_price()`.
check: VERIFY: Sturdy Finance (Aave V2 fork) used Curve stETH LP tokens as collateral, pricing them via Curve's get_virtual_price().
PROVE: Sturdy prices Curve stETH LP using get_virtual_price()
cards.antipattern: Sturdy prices Curve stETH LP using get_virtual_price()
cards.validWhen: Sturdy Finance (Aave V2 fork) used Curve stETH LP tokens as collateral, pricing them via Curve's get_virtual_price().

cards.title: 1. Fake Token Reward Harvesting Callback Reentrancy
cards.detect: When a protocol's batch reward harvesting function iterates over market pools and calls `rewardToken.claimRewards()` on...
check: VERIFY: When a protocol's batch reward harvesting function iterates over market pools and calls rewardToken.claimRewards() on each market's SY (Standardized...
PROVE: Deploy a fake SY token contract with a malicious claimRewards() function; Register the fake SY in a valid Pendle market (through market creation)
ANTIPATTERN: batchHarvestMarketRewards iterates markets calling claimRewards()

cards.title: 2. Empty Market Phantom Collateral Attack
cards.detect: In lending protocols, when a market is empty (no existing deposits), the first depositor sets the initial exchange rate.
check: VERIFY: In lending protocols, when a market is empty (no existing deposits), the first depositor sets the initial exchange rate.
PROVE: Flash loan SpookyV2 LP tokens; Deposit exactly 1e18 into the empty PolterFinance lending pool
ANTIPATTERN: No minimum deposit, empty market allows exchange rate manipulation
cards.antipattern: No minimum deposit, empty market allows exchange rate manipulation
cards.validWhen: In lending protocols, when a market is empty (no existing deposits), the first depositor sets the initial exchange rate.

cards.title: 3. ERC-3156 Flash Loan Callback Re-deposit Loop
cards.detect: ERC-3156 flash loans include an `onFlashLoan()` callback to the borrower.
check: VERIFY: ERC-3156 flash loans include an onFlashLoan() callback to the borrower.
PROVE: Request ERC-3156 flash loan from lending protocol; In onFlashLoan() callback: deposit the loaned tokens as collateral
ANTIPATTERN: Lending protocol's deposit() is callable during flash loan callback
cards.antipattern: Lending protocol's deposit() is callable during flash loan callback
cards.validWhen: ERC-3156 flash loans include an onFlashLoan() callback to the borrower.

cards.title: 4. Strategy Callback Reentrancy via Attacker-Controlled Hook
cards.detect: DEX protocols with customizable strategies allow liquidity providers to register callback hooks (e.
check: VERIFY: DEX protocols with customizable strategies allow liquidity providers to register callback hooks (e.g., burnHook, mintHook) that execute when...
PROVE: Deploy a malicious strategy contract that implements burnHook(); Add liquidity to a DEX pool using the malicious strategy
ANTIPATTERN: burnHook() calls attacker-controlled strategy contract
cards.antipattern: burnHook() calls attacker-controlled strategy contract
cards.validWhen: DEX protocols with customizable strategies allow liquidity providers to register callback hooks (e.g., burnHook, mintHook) that execute when positions are modified.

check: VERIFY: delegatedTransferERC20

cards.detect: Reference**: h-13-balancerpairoracle-can-be-manipulated-using-read-only-reentrancy.
check: VERIFY: Reference: h-13-balancerpairoracle-can-be-manipulated-using-read-only-reentrancy.

cards.title: 1. Merkle Proof Validation Failures
cards.detect: The Nomad Bridge Replica contract was incorrectly initialized with `committedRoot = 0x00`.
check: VERIFY: Nomad Bridge Replica contract was incorrectly initialized with committedRoot = 0x00.
PROVE: During a routine upgrade, the committedRoot was mistakenly set to 0x00; The process() function checks acceptableRoot(messages[_messageHash])
FALSIFY: Always require valid proof
IMPACT: Total Fund Drainage: All locked bridge funds can be withdrawn
cards.antipattern: Root can be set by attacker
cards.validWhen: The Nomad Bridge Replica contract was incorrectly initialized with committedRoot = 0x00.
cards.impact: Total Fund Drainage: All locked bridge funds can be withdrawn

cards.title: 2. Signature Verification Issues
cards.detect: Bridge signature verification may fail to properly validate signer addresses, allow signature reuse, or accept...
check: VERIFY: Bridge signature verification may fail to properly validate signer addresses, allow signature reuse, or accept malleable signatures.
PROVE: Same signer can sign multiple times
FALSIFY: Comprehensive message hashing
cards.antipattern: Same signer can sign multiple times
cards.validWhen: Bridge signature verification may fail to properly validate signer addresses, allow signature reuse, or accept malleable signatures.

cards.title: 3. Message Validation Bypasses
cards.detect: Poly Network's EthCrossChainManager
check: VERIFY: EthCrossChainManager contract could relay cross-chain messages to ANY contract, including the privileged EthCrossChainData contract.
PROVE: Poly Network's EthCrossChainManager
FALSIFY: Complete source validation
cards.antipattern: Poly Network's EthCrossChainManager
cards.validWhen: The EthCrossChainManager contract could relay cross-chain messages to ANY contract, including the privileged EthCrossChainData contract.

cards.title: 4. Centralization / Key Management Risks
cards.detect: Low validator/signer thresholds combined with insufficient operational security make key compromise a viable attack...
check: VERIFY: Low validator/signer thresholds combined with insufficient operational security make key compromise a viable attack vector.
PROVE: 5 of 9 threshold controlled by one entity
FALSIFY: Time delays for large withdrawals
cards.antipattern: 5 of 9 threshold controlled by one entity
cards.validWhen: Low validator/signer thresholds combined with insufficient operational security make key compromise a viable attack vector.

cards.title: 5. Token Validation in Bridge Deposits
cards.detect: Qubit's deposit handler
check: VERIFY: Qubit bridge handler called safeTransferFrom on address(0) for native token deposits.
PROVE: Anyswap's anySwapOutUnderlyingWithPermit
FALSIFY: Validate token implements expected interface
cards.antipattern: Anyswap's anySwapOutUnderlyingWithPermit
cards.validWhen: The Qubit bridge handler called safeTransferFrom on address(0) for native token deposits.

cards.title: 6. Replay Attacks Across Chains
cards.detect: Messages signed for one chain or bridge instance can potentially be replayed on another chain or instance if replay...
check: VERIFY: Messages signed for one chain or bridge instance can potentially be replayed on another chain or instance if replay...
FALSIFY: Full EIP-712 domain separation
ANTIPATTERN: Global nonce
cards.antipattern: Global nonce
cards.validWhen: Messages signed for one chain or bridge instance can potentially be replayed on another chain or instance if replay...

cards.title: 7. Arbitrary Call Vulnerabilities
cards.detect: Bridge aggregators accept user-provided calldata that is executed against arbitrary addresses.
check: VERIFY: Bridge aggregators accept user-provided calldata that is executed against arbitrary addresses.
PROVE: ChaingeFinance's swap function
FALSIFY: Users don't approve router directly
cards.antipattern: ChaingeFinance's swap function
cards.validWhen: Bridge aggregators accept user-provided calldata that is executed against arbitrary addresses.

cards.title: Understanding EIP-7702
cards.detect: EIP-7702, introduced with the Ethereum Pectra upgrade, fundamentally changes the EOA/contract distinction that many...
check: VERIFY: EIP-7702, introduced with the Ethereum Pectra upgrade, fundamentally changes the EOA/contract distinction that many...
cards.validWhen: EIP-7702, introduced with the Ethereum Pectra upgrade, fundamentally changes the EOA/contract distinction that many...

cards.title: 1. isContract/extcodesize Logic Broken
cards.detect: Smart contracts commonly use `isContract()` checks to determine whether to verify signatures using ECDSA (for EOAs) or...
check: VERIFY: Smart contracts commonly use isContract() checks to determine whether to verify signatures using ECDSA (for EOAs) or EIP-1271 isValidSignature() (for...
PROVE: User has an EOA that uses EIP-7702 to delegate to a smart wallet implementation; User signs a valid ECDSA signature for an order/action
FALSIFY: Check both ECDSA and EIP-1271 regardless of code presence
IMPACT: Signature verification logic breaks for EIP-7702 enabled accounts
Verify signature verification handles both ECDSA and EIP-1271
Check that isContract checks don't gate critical functionality
cards.antipattern: Relies on isContract to choose verification method
cards.securePattern: Check both ECDSA and EIP-1271 regardless of code presence
cards.validWhen: Smart contracts commonly use isContract() checks to determine whether to verify signatures using ECDSA (for EOAs) or EIP-1271 isValidSignature() (for contracts).
cards.invalidWhen: Check both ECDSA and EIP-1271 regardless of code presence
cards.impact: Signature verification logic breaks for EIP-7702 enabled accounts

cards.title: 2. Reentrancy via EIP-7702 Delegated EOAs
cards.detect: Protocols that detect EIP-7702 delegated wallets may use low-level `.call` with unlimited gas to send ETH, enabling...
check: VERIFY: Protocols that detect EIP-7702 delegated wallets may use low-level .call with unlimited gas to send ETH, enabling complex operations in the...
PROVE: Attacker sets up an EIP-7702 EOA delegating to malicious contract code; Protocol detects the 7702 delegation and uses .call with unlimited gas to send ETH
FALSIFY: Limit gas regardless of recipient type
IMPACT: Reentrancy attacks possible from addresses that appear as EOAs
Verify all external calls are protected by reentrancy guards
Check that CEI pattern is followed consistently
cards.antipattern: Unlimited gas call to 7702 wallet enables reentrancy
cards.securePattern: Limit gas regardless of recipient type
cards.validWhen: Protocols that detect EIP-7702 delegated wallets may use low-level .call with unlimited gas to send ETH, enabling complex operations in the recipient's receive/fallback...
cards.invalidWhen: Limit gas regardless of recipient type
cards.impact: Reentrancy attacks possible from addresses that appear as EOAs

cards.title: 3. Missing Token Receiver Callbacks
cards.detect: EIP-7702 smart wallet implementations that don't implement `IERC721Receiver` and `IERC1155Receiver` interfaces will...
check: VERIFY: EIP-7702 smart wallet implementations that don't implement IERC721Receiver and IERC1155Receiver interfaces will fail to receive NFTs via safeTransfer...
PROVE: User upgrades their EOA to use an EIP-7702 smart wallet delegation; User attempts to receive an NFT via safeTransferFrom
FALSIFY: Inherit from comprehensive handler
IMPACT: EIP-7702 enabled wallets cannot receive NFTs via safeTransfer
Verify EIP-7702 delegate contracts implement IERC721Receiver
Check for IERC1155Receiver implementation
cards.antipattern: Only implements ERC-721, not ERC-1155
cards.validWhen: EIP-7702 smart wallet implementations that don't implement IERC721Receiver and IERC1155Receiver interfaces will fail to receive NFTs via safeTransfer functions.
cards.impact: EIP-7702 enabled wallets cannot receive NFTs via safeTransfer

cards.title: 4. Cross-Chain Address Mismatch
cards.detect: Smart contract wallets are deployed using factory contracts with chain-specific parameters (deployment salts, nonces,...
check: VERIFY: Smart contract wallets are deployed using factory contracts with chain-specific parameters (deployment salts, nonces, factory addresses).
PROVE: User Alice uses a Safe wallet at address 0xAAA...111 on Ethereum; Alice bridges tokens to Polygon via a protocol that uses msg.sender as destination
FALSIFY: User specifies destination address explicitly
IMPACT: Tokens/assets sent to wrong addresses on destination chains
Verify bridge functions allow explicit destination addresses
Check that users can specify different addresses per chain
cards.antipattern: Assumes msg.sender is same on destination chain
cards.validWhen: Smart contract wallets are deployed using factory contracts with chain-specific parameters (deployment salts, nonces, factory addresses).
cards.impact: Tokens/assets sent to wrong addresses on destination chains

cards.title: 5. tx.origin Exploitation with Smart Wallets
cards.detect: Smart wallets like Gnosis Safe accept signed transactions via `execTransaction()` and execute them on behalf of signers.
check: VERIFY: Smart wallets like Gnosis Safe accept signed transactions via execTransaction() and execute them on behalf of signers.
PROVE: User with Smart Wallet signs a transaction to create a dispute game with bond; Transaction is submitted to mempool calling Safe's execTransaction()
FALSIFY: Use msg.sender for identification
IMPACT: Rewards/bonds assigned to attackers instead of legitimate users
Search for all tx.origin usages in codebase
Verify tx.origin is never used for authorization
cards.antipattern: Rewards go to tx.origin instead of actual caller
cards.validWhen: Smart wallets like Gnosis Safe accept signed transactions via execTransaction() and execute them on behalf of signers.
cards.impact: Rewards/bonds assigned to attackers instead of legitimate users

cards.title: 6. Fallback/Receive Function Issues
cards.detect: EIP-7702 transforms an EOA into what appears to be a smart contract.
check: VERIFY: EIP-7702 transforms an EOA into what appears to be a smart contract.
PROVE: Protocol sends ETH to an address using .call{value: amount}(""); Address is an EIP-7702 EOA delegating to implementation without payable fallback
FALSIFY: Both functions handle ETH properly
IMPACT: EIP-7702 wallets cannot receive ETH through standard call patterns
Verify EIP-7702 implementations have payable fallback
Check receive() function exists and is payable
cards.antipattern: fallback cannot receive ETH
cards.validWhen: EIP-7702 transforms an EOA into what appears to be a smart contract.
cards.impact: EIP-7702 wallets cannot receive ETH through standard call patterns

cards.title: 7. Session Key Impersonation in Smart Wallets
cards.detect: When a smart wallet allows multiple active sessions concurrently, the signature validation must ensure that the session...
check: VERIFY: When a smart wallet allows multiple active sessions concurrently, the signature validation must ensure that the session key that signed the...
PROVE: Smart Wallet (SCW) has multiple active session keys (SK1, SK2); SK2 signs a message for the claim() function, but passes SK1 as the sessionKey parameter
FALSIFY: Extract sessionKey from calldata and verify against signer
IMPACT: Unauthorized consumption of session key allowances
Verify session key in calldata matches the signing session key
Check that validateUserOp binds the signer to the operation
cards.antipattern: claim() uses sessionKey from parameter, not from signer
cards.securePattern: Extract sessionKey from calldata and verify against signer
cards.validWhen: When a smart wallet allows multiple active sessions concurrently, the signature validation must ensure that the session key that signed the transaction is the same one...
cards.invalidWhen: Extract sessionKey from calldata and verify against signer
cards.impact: Unauthorized consumption of session key allowances

cards.title: 8. NFT Permit Function Exploitation
cards.detect: ERC-4494 adds a `permit()` function to ERC-721, allowing off-chain signed approvals.
check: VERIFY: ERC-4494 adds a permit() function to ERC-721, allowing off-chain signed approvals.
PROVE: Attacker rents an NFT, which is held in their rental safe; Attacker creates a permit signature to approve themselves as spender
FALSIFY: Check for permit support before accepting NFT
IMPACT: Bypass of transfer restrictions on held tokens
Check if held NFTs support ERC-4494 (permit)
Verify isValidSignature blocks dangerous operations
cards.antipattern: Wallet owner can sign permit for tokens held by wallet
cards.securePattern: Check for permit support before accepting NFT
cards.validWhen: ERC-4494 adds a permit() function to ERC-721, allowing off-chain signed approvals.
cards.invalidWhen: Check for permit support before accepting NFT
cards.impact: Bypass of transfer restrictions on held tokens
